The track and field competition at the2024 Paris Olympic Gameswas one of the most anticipated segments of the Olympics.

The multitude of events were held in and around Stade de France in Paris from Aug. 1-Aug. 11.

Every competition had its favorites, and fans around the world tuned in at all hours to see how it all was going to shake out.

Search bars were being populated with names such asSha'carri Richardson,Noah Lyles,Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone,Sherika Jackson,Quincy Wilson,Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce,Armand Mondo Duplantis,Sifan Hassanand many more.

Here's a look at every 2024 Olympic track and field athlete by country.

But how did they do? Did they deliver as expected? Of course, there were upsets, and new stars emerged, too.

See how each country stacked up in Paris:

Through 11 days of track and field activity at the 2024 Olympic Games, the United States topped the track and field medal table with 34 medals - 14 gold, 11 silver and nine bronze. It was the most track and field medals for Team USA since the 1984 Olympics in Los Angeles and the first time in history the United States swept the hurdle events.

The first gold went toRyan Crouser, who claimed his third consecutive Olympic gold medal in the men's shot put, whileNoah Lylesended the Aug. 4 action by beating Jamaica's Kishane Thompson by .005 seconds for the gold in the men's 100m.

Valarie Allmanadded Team USA's third gold medal with a win in the women's discus Aug. 5. It was her second consecutive victory in the event, this time with toss of 69.50 meters. She became the fourth woman in history to win two Olympic gold medals in discus and second to do it in back-to-back Olympics.

On Aug. 6, Team USA picked up a pair of medals in the men's 1,500 and then the women's 200, winning gold and bronze in both.

In the men's 1,500,Cole Hockerstunned the favorites - Jakob Ingerbrigtsen and Josh Kerr - to take the gold medal with an Olympic-record 3:27.65 finish.It nearly was an American record, too.

While Kerr nabbed the silver, AmericanYared Nugusefinished strong to take the bronze, denying defending champion Ingerbrigtsen a spot on the podium.

The women's 200 closed the action for the day in Paris, withGabby Thomascruising into the top spot with a 21.83 effort. St. Lucia's Julien Alfred, who was trying to win the coveted 100-200 double, settled for the silver (22.08). Team USA's Brittany Brown took the bronze (22.20).

In the men's 400 on Aug. 7, Quincy Hall went from gassed to gold, using the final 100 meters to surge from fourth place to the coveted title.

The golds for Team USA on Aug. 8 came from Grant Holloway in the men's 110-meter hurdles, Sydney McLauglin-Levrone in the women's 400-meter hurdles and Tara Davis-Woodhall in the women's long jump.

Team USA shined in Paris on Aug. 9, making statement wins in the women's 4x100 and the men's 400-meter hurdles. The day included redemption wins for Richardson, who just missed the gold in the women's 100, along with Rai Benjamin, who took silver in the 400-meter hurdles in Tokyo.

Richardson was joined in the relay win by Melissa Jefferson, Twanisha Terry and Thomas.

The final full day of track and field competition Aug. 10 brought Team USA three more gold medals - women's 100-meter hurdles (Masai Russell) and both 4x400 relays. The men won the 4x400 for the third consecutive time, while women continued their dominance, winning for the eighth consecutive time. This year, the women were just shy of a world record.

Two of the early silver medals for Team USA came in what likely would be considered huge upsets.

First, a jaw-dropping final leg helped the Netherlands past Team USA in the 4x400 mixed relay. The other came from Alfred, who outlasted slow-starting favorite Richardson in the women's 100m.

Sam Kendricks' silver medal in the men's pole vault saw him finish behind superstar Armand Duplantis and his world-record leap of 6.25 meters. Kendricks managed 5.95.

Team USA's other silver medals came from:

The bronze medals for the United States came in the women's 100m (Jefferson), women's triple jump (Jasmine Moore), men's 10,000m (Grant Fisher), men's 100m (Fred Kerley), men's 1,500 (Nuguse), women's 200 (Brown), men's 200 (Lyles), women's long jump (Moore) and the men's 5,000 (Fisher).

Here's a look at the medal count for the Paris Olympic Game track and field meet:

Total medals:145

The Team USA men won the 4x400 for the third consecutive time. The lineup for the final included Chris Bailey, Vernon Norwood, Bryce Deadmon and Rai Benjamin. Quincy Wilson, a 16-year-old phenom earns a gold medal, too, after running in the preliminary round.

The women's lineup for the final featured Shamier Little, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone, Gabby Thomas and Alexis Holmes. Quanera Hayes, Aaliyah Butler and Kaylyn Brown also were part of the effort this week.

The women's win was their eighth consecutive Olympic gold medal.

An American Record and a Gold for Team USA 🇺🇲We'll take that combo any day of the week 💪The team of Shamier Little, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone, Gabby Thomas and Alexis Holmes runs an unbelievable 3:15.27 and misses the World Record by a tenth of a second.#Paris2024📸Gettypic.twitter.com/WNWmPhoHPt

Even rain couldn't stop the Team USA women from taking gold in Paris.

SHA'CARRI RICHARDSON WALKS DOWN THE FIELD! 🇺🇸Team USA was right around fourth position going into the final leg and it was a rough exchange, but Richardson produces an epic anchor to help the women to a 4x100m title in 41.78.Reported Splits:Melissa Jefferson - 11.46TeeTee…pic.twitter.com/uvuEKtRtgG

Is Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone the undisputed queen?

👑👑👑The U.S. champion and undisputed champion of the world continued her reign in the 400mH finals on Thursday at the Paris Olympics as Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone lowered her own world record for gold#Paris2024https://t.co/POVxVsu707

How did Team USA's Quincy Hall go from down and out to the top of the podium in the men's 400 at the 2024 Olympics in Paris? Here's how:

Quincy Hall's vs Matthew Hudson-Smith's 100m Splits🇺🇸 Hall's Splits - 11.01, 9.99, 10.81, 11.59 = 43.40🇬🇧 Hudson-Smith - 10.81, 9.81, 10.73, 12.09 = 43.44Everyone's Splits Over the Final 100m?11.59 🇺🇸 Quincy Hall12.09 🇬🇧 Matthew Hudson-Smith11.83 🇿🇲 Muzala…pic.twitter.com/h08hRydRi9

Hocker wins in Paris with Olympic record and nearly gets American record, too!

Cole Hocker NEARLY gets the American Record in Paris. The American All-Time 1500m List has received a serious shake-up 📈Top 10 Americans of All-Time 🇺🇸3:27.40 - Bernard Lagat (Nike) 8/28/053:27.65 - Cole Hocker (Nike) 8/6/243:27.80 -…pic.twitter.com/hWIK37ENUw

Sweden's Mondo Duplantis won the men's pole vault in Paris with a record jump of 6.25 meters.

🐐How many goats can The GOAT vault over? 13!Mondo Duplantis cleared 6.25m to break his own world record again and win the Olympic title.That's about 20 feet & 6 inches, or 13 average sized goats.📸 Getty |#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/SgNMF8bfww

See how the men's 100 turned out Sunday in Paris. Wowza!

Read more:Olympic Track & Field Live Updates And Results For Aug. 4 Evening Session

Noah Lyles tied for having the slowest reaction time of anyone in the field, but he still won the men's 100m final...REACTION TIMES:Fred Kerley 🇺🇸 - 0.108Marcell Jacobs 🇮🇹 - 0.114Akani Simbine 🇿🇦 - 0.149Kenny Bednarek 🇺🇸 - 0.163Oblique Seville 🇯🇲 - 0.171Kishane Thompson…pic.twitter.com/mqiufabp9l

Thanks to an unreal final leg by Femke Bol, the Netherlands was able to edge Team USA for the Olympic gold medal in the 4x400 relay.

Read more:2024 Olympics Track & Field - Day 2 Recap

47.93 ON THE ANCHOR SPLIT FROM FEMKE BOL 😱An UNREAL final leg from the Netherlands 🇳🇱 as they narrowly beat out the newly-crowned world record holders from the U.S. in the mixed 4x400m relay.They clock 3:07.43 for the win.🥈 USA - 3:07.74🥉 Great Britain - 3:08.01…pic.twitter.com/tGYLGwiXJI

The men's 10,000-meter event at the 2024 Olympics in Paris was one for the ages. It was a tale of redemption for champion Joshua Cheptegei of Uganda that included an Olympic record.

See who claimed the medals:

🏆 GOLD for Joshua CheptegeiIt only took an Olympic Record to win it! Joshua Cheptegei held off the field and won the 10,000m title for Uganda after finishing second in Tokyo three years ago. 26:43.14.Berihu Aregawi takes silver for Ethiopia, and Grant Fisher makes the United…pic.twitter.com/grERCUQ72t

Ecuador's Brian Daniel Pintado wins the first track and field gold medal of the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, winning the men's 20-kilometer race walk. It also was the first gold medal for Ecuador overall at the 2024 Olympics.

Yang Jiayu of the People's Republic of China won on the women's side.

#GOLD! 🇪🇨Brian Daniel Pintado takes the first medal on Day 6, earning the win for Ecuador in the men’s 20km race walk.It’s their first gold of the Games, and their fourth ever in Olympic history. 🎉@ECUADORolimpico|@WorldAthletics|#Athletics|#Paris2024|#Samsung|…pic.twitter.com/FHVce1Nstq

The Olympics were broadcast on the NBC family of networks, including the Peacock streaming app.

FloTrack was on the ground in Paris with the latest news. Check out all the news and notes and vibes from Paris on FloTrack.

Don't miss all the track and field season action streaming on FloTrack.Check out the FloTrack schedule for more events.

Video footage from each event will be archived and stored in a video library for FloTrack subscribers to watch for the duration of their subscriptions.

title: 2024 Olympics Medals Tracker For Track And Field: See Who Won Gold - FloTrack
description: Check out the track and field medal count for the 2024 Olympics in Paris. Competition was held at (and around) Stade de France from Aug. 1-11.
